channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Risks of Retaliating Against U.S. Tariffs Through Oil Export Cuts
item.description: Heather Exner-Pirot argues that Ontario Premier Doug Ford's threat to cut oil exports to retaliate against Donald Trump's tariffs could backfire catastrophically. Canada's economy is deeply intertwine...

item.title: Maluti-a-Phofong Municipality Faces Salary Delays Amid Financial Mismanagement and Treasury Disputes
item.description: The Maluti-a-Phofong Local Municipality has delayed July salary payments to its staff, citing withheld equitable share funds from the National Treasury as the primary reason. This follows a letter sen...

item.title: Legal challenge over prison consultation rules in Tanzania
item.description: Lawyers representing Chadema leader Tundu Lissu have filed a petition challenging prison consultation rules, arguing they violate constitutional rights to private legal communication. The case, heard ...

item.title: Philippine Stock Market Rally Driven by Fundamentals and Foreign Investments
item.description: The article discusses the current state of the Philippine stock market, highlighting a shift in investor sentiment due to geopolitical risks. While short-term volatility is driven by fears of Middle E...

item.title: Jehan Georges Vibert: French Illustrator Known for Satirical Ecclesiastical Art
item.description: Jehan Georges Vibert (1840–1902) was a renowned French academic painter and illustrator celebrated for his witty, satirical depictions of ecclesiastical life. His work blended meticulous technical ski...

item.title: UK Government Expands No 10 North Office with Plans for Regional Staff Relocation
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham's plan to establish a regional government office in Manchester, known as No 10 North, is gaining momentum. Senior ministers and aides are set to work from this new location...

item.title: GOP Senators Criticize Trump's Pressure on Senate Leader Over Voting Bill
item.description: President Donald Trump has intensified his criticism of Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD), accusing him of failing to pass the SAVE America Act, a citizenship-proof voting bill. At a rally in G...

item.title: Mark Carney Clarifies Terms of U.S.-Canada Bridge Deal After Contradictions
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's former economic advisor, Mark Carney, admitted he could have explained the details of the Canada-U.S. bridge deal better after initial contradictions arose between his ...

item.title: Advocate Challenges Judge's Conduct During Matlala Trial
item.description: During the trial of Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala, his legal representative Advocate Annelene van den Heever confronted Judge Cassim Moosa over his dismissive body language during cross-examination. The advo...

item.title: Banks Drop InstaPay Transfer Fees as Costs Reduced
item.description: This July, major banks in the Philippines have significantly reduced or completely eliminated InstaPay transfer fees, which previously ranged up to P15 per transaction. The shift marks a pivotal chang...

item.title: Global Music Labels Plan Nairobi Expansion to Strengthen Kenya’s Creative Industry
item.description: Kenya’s music industry is expected to receive a significant boost following an announcement by President William Ruto that three of the world’s largest music companies—Sony Music, Universal Music Grou...

item.title: Chopard's Family Business Transition from Wine to Luxury Watches
item.description: The article explores the transition of the Chopard family business from the wine industry to luxury watchmaking. Karl-Fritz Scheufele, a 28-year-old heir to the Chopard watch company, previously manag...

item.title: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ends hunger strike after 26 days amid political demands
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who had been on a 26-day hunger strike, ended his fast at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram with the presence of Union ministers Jitendra Singh and JP Nadda. The protest, led by th...

item.title: China's Plan to Deflect Asteroids with High-Speed Spacecraft
item.description: China's National Space Administration is planning a planetary defense test that aims to alter an asteroid's trajectory by striking it with a spacecraft traveling at Mach 26. This mission, set for 2029...

item.title: Andy Burnham's Devolution Plan to Revitalize the UK Economy
item.description: The article outlines Andy Burnham's initiative, 'No 10 North,' aimed at decentralizing power and fostering regional growth across the UK. It critiques decades of centralization and deindustrialisation...

item.title: DepEd and LGUs Collaborate to Address Nationwide Classroom Shortage
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has partnered with all local government units (LGUs) to tackle the nationwide classroom shortage, estimated at 144,000 units. Undersecretary Filemon Ray Javier emph...

item.title: PM Modi Announces Stricter Measures Against Paper Leaks and New Bill
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced stricter measures against examination paper leaks, including the introduction of a new bill to be discussed in the Union Cabinet. The government aims to esta...

item.title: Tanzania's Peace Initiatives and Tourism Boost
item.description: The article highlights two key developments in Tanzania. First, the African Alliance for Freedom and Progress (AAFP) has called for national unity and peace to drive the country's development. This co...

item.title: PM Modi Announces Stricter Measures Against Paper Leaks Amid CJP Protests
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has announced stricter measures against paper leaks during a cabinet meeting, addressing concerns over the NEET-UG 2026 exam leak that affected over 22 lakh students. The ...

item.title: Zebrafish Regenerate Gut Neurons in Nine Days, Offering Clues for Nerve Repair
item.description: Researchers at Michigan State University have developed a groundbreaking method to study nerve regeneration by observing zebrafish regenerate damaged gut neurons within nine days. The study, published...
